author: Sundar Singh, Bhai
title: Upkari Jiwan
gloss: The story of a Sikh lady who taught her female friends and imparted to them teachings from the religious scriptures of the Sikhs.

author: Vasakha Singh, Bhai
title: Nai Prabodh arthat naiyan nun subh sikhiya
gloss: The story of a villager and a barber in illustration of the brutal treatment the barbers receive at the hands of those whom they serve.
notes: Published by a barber about barbers' mistreatment — occupational-caste grievance in print; author Vasakha Singh (later a Ghadar figure).

title: Bal Bodh
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. A primer in the Mahajani (merchant shorthand) script — rare script attestation; only 350 copies.

author: Durga Das, B.A., LL.B.
title: Punjabi-Hindi di Pahli Pothi
gloss: Or the First Book of Punjabi in the Hindi character.
publisher: Brij Lal, Secretary, Vedic Putri Pathshahla
notes: Section carries '(Designed for educational purposes.)'. Punjabi taught in Devanagari from an Arya Samaj girls' school (Vedic Putri Pathshala) — script politics: Punjabi language in Hindi script. Serial 2 continues Punjabi—Language across the script subsection.

author: Thakar Singh, Bhai
title: Punjabi di Pahli Katab
notes: Punjabi primer for soldiers by a serving sepoy of the 14th Sikhs — army literacy pipeline (cf. the sepoy-author primers of 1910Q4).

title: The Anand Marriage Act, VII of 1909, with extracts from speeches and their Punjabi translation, with comments, etc.
gloss: The Anand Marriage Act, VII of 1909, with extracts from speeches and their Punjabi translation, with comments, etc.
copyright: Bhai Bahadur Singh, Manager, Wazir-i-Hind Press, Amritsar; No. 2, dated the 7th February, 1910.
notes: The Anand Marriage Act (1909) — the landmark law legalizing the distinct Sikh marriage rite (Anand Karaj), a key Tat Khalsa identity marker — annotated in Punjabi within months of passage; central homogenization/identity-formation document.
